question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user use the /outstanding endpoint with a receiverIds parameter set to a range covering all possible account IDs to force a full scan of the TOKEN_AIRDROP table filtered only by senderAccountId, consuming all available database I/O bandwidth and preventing gossip transactions from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user use the /pending endpoint with a senderIds parameter set to a range covering all possible account IDs to force a full scan of the TOKEN_AIRDROP table filtered only by receiverAccountId, consuming all available database I/O bandwidth and preventing gossip transactions from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the three-part entity ID format (shard.realm.num) in EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf() to supply a shard or realm value that differs from the network's configured shard/realm, causing the lookup to resolve to a different entity namespace and leaking cross-shard airdrop transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityIdRangeParameter.getEntityId() uses CommonProperties.getInstance() (a singleton) to resolve default shard/realm, and by sending requests with mismatched shard values, cause the service to query for airdrops belonging to a different network shard, leaking gossip transaction metadata?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum value constraint on serialNumbers in NumberRangeParameter to supply Long.MAX_VALUE as a serial number, causing the getInclusiveValue() method to overflow when operator is GT (value + 1 wraps to Long.MIN_VALUE), generating an invalid SQL condition that causes a database error and disrupts gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a minimum value constraint on serialNumbers in NumberRangeParameter to supply 0 as a serial number with LT operator, causing getInclusiveValue() to return -1 (value - 1), generating a negative serial number condition that may bypass the AirdropState.PENDING filter and expose cancelled or claimed airdrop transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user use the pagination next-link mechanism in TokenAirdropsController to continuously iterate through all pending airdrop transactions without any cursor expiry or rate limiting, enabling complete enumeration of all gossip-eligible transactions in the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getSort() logic in TokenAirdropsController, where a null or zero serialNumber causes the sort to omit SERIAL_NUMBER from the next-link sort fields, to craft a pagination cursor that skips NFT airdrop transactions in the result set, causing those transactions to be invisible to gossip monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EXTRACTOR function in TokenAirdropsController, which uses DEFAULT_SERIAL_NUMBER '0L' when serialNumber is null, to craft a next-link that incorrectly anchors pagination at serial number 0, causing the next page to re-fetch already-seen airdrop transactions and creating an infinite loop that exhausts server resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ImmutableSortedMap used in the EXTRACTOR to inject a crafted tokenId or senderId value containing special characters that, when embedded in the next-link URL, cause URL parsing errors in downstream clients and prevent those clients from tracking gossip transaction state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl.findAll() uses DSLContext.selectFrom(TOKEN_AIRDROP) without any row-level security or tenant isolation to access airdrop transactions belonging to any account on the network, enabling full enumeration of all pending gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the STATE filter in findAll() is hardcoded to AirdropState.PENDING to confirm whether a specific airdrop transaction has been gossipped and processed (by observing its disappearance from the PENDING state), enabling timing-based inference of gossip propagation speed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of request deduplication in getAirdrops() to send thousands of identical requests simultaneously, causing the database to execute the same expensive query in parallel and creating a thundering herd that prevents gossip transaction writes from acquiring database locks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of query result caching in TokenAirdropServiceImpl to repeatedly query for the same accountId's pending airdrops, bypassing any potential cache layer and forcing repeated database hits that degrade gossip transaction processing throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound constructor's validation logic, which only throws IllegalArgumentException when primarySortField is true and adjustedLower > adjustedUpper, to craft a non-primary Bound with an inverted range (lower > upper) that generates an impossible SQL condition, causing the query to return zero results and masking pending gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that serialNumbers Bound is constructed with primarySortField=false in TokenAirdropsController (line 103), meaning inverted serial number ranges are not validated, to supply a serialNumbers range where lower > upper and silently receive an empty result set that hides pending NFT airdrop gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Size(max = 2) constraint on receiverIds/senderIds/serialNumbers/tokenIds parameters to supply exactly 2 parameters with the same operator (e.g., two GT operators), bypassing the verifySingleOccurrence() check that is never called in the controller flow, and generating an ambiguous SQL condition that causes incorrect filtering of gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySingleOccurrence() and verifyEqualOrRange() are defined in Bound but never called in the TokenAirdropService flow to supply both an EQ and a GT operator for the same parameter, creating a contradictory SQL condition that either returns no results or causes a database error, preventing gossip transaction visibility?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Bound constructor iterates over all params and sets lower/upper based on hasLowerBound()/hasUpperBound() without checking for duplicate assignments, so that supplying two lower-bound parameters causes the second to silently overwrite the first, creating an inconsistent Bound state that generates incorrect SQL and hides pending gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the split(':') parsing in NumberRangeParameter.valueOf() to supply a serial number like 'gt:9223372036854775807' (Long.MAX_VALUE) with GT operator, causing getInclusiveValue() to return Long.MIN_VALUE due to overflow, generating a negative serial number SQL condition that bypasses the intended filter and exposes gossip transactions with unexpected serial numbers?
